pages.raw_text:
Another letter from Lynn, Mass.e
states that II. L. Hughes Of Spokapx
is making considerable Roadway II
the cause Of the American Labet
union. The communication says:


'Mr Hughes spoke before the LynA
Labor council, February 6; February
8 at a meeting of the B. and 8. p. u.
Of Lynn; the following Tuesday at
the grain counter workers; Wednes-
day, Counter Workers, No. 262, Hav-
erhill Mass, and Thursday at a
mass meeting of the Heel Workers,
No. 1853. His addresses did more
good in the two hours he spoke than
any other labor leader has accom-
plished."


AS there were only six present at
the meeting of the elevator boys held
Saturday evening at the municipal
court for the purpose of forming a
union, the work of organizing had to
be postponed until Thursday. 1t is
necessary to have 10 members in or
der to get charter from the Ameri-
can Labor union, and nearly all the
80 elevator boys in the city have
signified their willingness to join the
union.


w. II. Upper of the typographical
union advises all unions in Spokane
to affiliate with the Washington
State Federation Of Labor. He says:
"At the present time there are but
IA out of AZ unions in Spokane affili-
ated with the state federation. The
others should now como in and help
the work along and not stay on the
| outside, selfishly reaping the benefits
of their fellow workers who are
spending their energy and funds to
effect a solidarity of labor"
| The bridge and structural iron
workers union held it's regular meet
ing at Central Labor hall Saturday
night. There was good attendance.
Twelve new members were initiated.
The union has been very prosperous
and the officers say the outlook is
encouraging.


The American Federation Of Labor
made record in December. 1t grant
ed charters to 16 central bodies, AZ
local trades unions, 29 federal unions
and to the telegraphers and glove
workers.
